_publications._publicationTitle: Quantitative MRI of dorsal root ganglion alterations in neurofibromatosis type 1 patients with or without pain.

_contextSnippet: For discriminating between pain and non-pain phenotype in NF1 patients, the DRG PD parameter (AUC = 0.84; Fig. 4) was superior to DRG volume (AUC = 0.72) and DRG T2 (AUC = 0.71)
details: Successfully performed logistic regression analysis showing DRG PD as superior discriminator (AUC = 0.84) between pain and non-pain NF1 phenotypes with sensitivity 0.75 and specificity 0.92 at threshold 0.918 a.u.
